title: Mid session 2901 T225 D
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . STUDENT NUMBER: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS

Term 2, 2025

MATH2901 Higher Theory of Statistics Mid-session test D

(1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

(2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

(3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

(4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

(5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

(6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided. You may use your own calculators. You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask if you need more paper.

All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.  Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 2

1. [10 marks] A standard deck of cards is shuffled and n cards are drawn with replacement (meaning, you put each card back in the deck and shuffle before the next draw). Let X be the number of hearts drawn and Y be the number of spades drawn.

a) [5 marks] Find the covariance between X and Y .

(Hint: Write X = ni=1 Xi and Y = nj=1 Yj , where Xi = 1I{heart at the ith draw} P P and Yj = 1I{spade at the j th draw} )

b) [2 marks] The covariance that you obtain in a) should be negative. Explain intuitively why.

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 3

c) [3 marks] Find the variance of the total number of hearts or spades balls drawn; that is, Var(X + Y ).

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 4

2. [10 marks] Let (X, Y ) be a continuous bivariate vector with joint density

fXY (x, y) = c1I{0≤x≤2,0≤y≤1,x+y≤2}

for some constant c > 0.

a) [2 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.

b) [2 marks] Are X and Y independent? Justify.

c) [3 marks] Find the marginal density of Y .

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 5

d) [3 marks] Find the conditional density fX|Y (x|y). Name this distribution (i.e., the conditional distribution of X given Y = y), and deduce the conditional expectation E(X|Y = y).

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 6

3. [10 marks] A pharmaceutical company claims that their new medication reduces recovery time from a certain illness. Historical data shows that without medication, recovery time has mean 12 days and standard deviation 3 days.

a) [6 marks] Suppose that n = 36 patients are given the new medication and their average recovery time is observed. Assuming the medication has no effect, provide an approximation for the probability that the average recovery time is less than 10.5 days. (Hint: Φ(1.5) = 0.9332, Φ(2.0) = 0.9772, Φ(2.5) = 0.9938, Φ(3.0) = 0.9987)

b) [2 marks] Do you think that the previous approximation is accurate? Explain briefly.

c) [2 marks] If the observed average recovery time is indeed 10.5 days, does this provide evidence that the medication is effective? Explain.
